advance_conditions.description: continued uninterrupted Bourse-coin acceptance at major exchange points
successful arbitration of at least one major inter-faction commercial dispute
resolution of Iron Crown chit devaluation without regional crisis

advance_rate.description: fast (estimated 1 segment per 3–6 months at T+1)

completion_consequences.description: Bourse copper becomes the formal regional standard
continuity scrip trades at a fixed (unfavorable) rate against copper
trade formalizes; contract law tied to Bourse arbitration spreads
House prestige realigns around copper-settlement capacity

reset_conditions.description: Bourse house schism or major default
South Philly Holding turns on Bourse

interactions.description: completion reduces probability of Iron Crown expansion (economic isolation)
completion accelerates Species I consolidation trajectory

bourse-currency-stability.narrative_description: The copper coin the merchants stamp is already doing the work of a standard; what remains is the final recognition. Two-thirds of mid-Atlantic trade already settles in Bourse copper. The last segment is the Lehigh Principalities signing a multi-year grain-for-coal contract denominated in copper, not barter. After that, the currency is simply what money is.

advance_conditions.description: diffusion of saturation-yield techniques through remaining biokinetic practitioners
establishment of training programs in new regions (Delmarva, Central NJ already complete)

advance_rate.description: fast (Rogers S-curve past inflection; estimated 6–12 months per segment)

completion_consequences.description: regional food surplus becomes permanent
famine risk ends across mid-Atlantic (except in faction-collapse scenarios)
biokinetic-healer wages decouple from biokinetic-farmer wages (specialization hardens)
migration pressure into farm districts abates

reset_conditions.description: climate shift or pest outbreak faster than diffusion

interactions.description: completion reduces faction-collapse probability across most scenarios
completion makes Delmarva Harvest Lords relatively more powerful

biokinetic-ag-saturation.narrative_description: The technique is simple once learned. A biokinetic farmer who has seen it done can teach five apprentices in a season. Three years after the first Delmarva demonstration, most of the region's biokinetic farmers are using some version. Three more segments and the grain surplus is a structural fact. Nobody will starve through a bad winter again, unless the world breaks in some other way.

advance_conditions.description: failed Fed Continuity expansion attempts (Baltimore failure already advanced this)

completion_consequences.description: Colonel Reeve's Potomac Irregulars formally declare independence
Fed Continuity reduces to DC Mall / Pentagon enclave
continuity scrip collapses; holders face catastrophic losses
Virginia District territory reorganizes under Irregular rule
Species I Consolidation clock accelerates (Fed Continuity was an anchor for status quo)

reset_conditions.description: major Fed Continuity military victory (extremely unlikely)

interactions.description: completion shifts Species I clock forward
completion de-anchors Bourse-Continuity scrip arbitrage

fed-continuity-erosion.narrative_description: The institution was built on the fiction that the country continued. The longer no citizen outside DC sees a federal officer, the thinner the fiction gets. Colonel Reeve in Virginia acts as if Alcott's orders are recommendations. The failed Baltimore expedition proved the reach isn't there. Half the clock has filled in a year. The rest will not take as long.

advance_conditions.description: failed mediation attempts (Bourse has tried twice)

completion_consequences.description: open war over Manhattan salvage access
Newark-Staten Island harbor becomes a combat zone
major disruption of salvage trade (Tower Lords affected heavily)
likely outcome favors Staten due to spatial-teleportation capability
Iron Crown succession crisis if Volk killed or humiliated

reset_conditions.description: negotiated Bourse-backed toll framework with formal adjustment mechanism
external threat forcing both to defensive postures

interactions.description: completion hurts Bourse commercial volume
completion indirectly empowers Tower Lords (less Iron Crown pressure)
Volk death from war could accelerate fragmentation of Iron Crown territory

iron-crown-vs-staten.narrative_description: It is a tariff dispute shaped like a feud. Viktor Chen holds the harbor; Volk wants Manhattan. The Bourse has negotiated twice and both times Volk broke the terms within a season. Three segments of six. The captains all know which way it's going. What none of them know is whether Volk survives it.

advance_conditions.description: Listening cult growth (SIS model indicates steady expansion)
Sprawl territorial creep past defensive boundaries
successful Listening proselytization in populated areas

advance_rate.description: slow (1 segment per 1–2 years at current rate)

completion_consequences.description: The Listening breaches the Delaware line
populated southern NJ townships enter corruption contact
potential collapse of Central Jersey League southern townships
Species I Reyes-kin directly threatened
regional existential response required

reset_conditions.description: successful coordinated faction strike against Listening core
One Who Waits death or dispersal

interactions.description: completion resets most other clocks (regional crisis)
completion may trigger alliance formations not otherwise possible

pine-barrens-expansion.narrative_description: Twelve segments was a generous estimate. The cult grows by a few thousand each season. The Sprawl creeps outward a field a year. One Who Waits speaks through messengers once or twice a year. The clock moves slowly, but every segment is a measure of how much land is quietly passing from factions to something else.

advance_conditions.description: Fed Continuity expansion attempts into Baltimore region (one failure so far)

completion_consequences.description: open war along Chesapeake line
disruption of Delmarva–Crabclaw–Bourse trade
likely Continuity withdrawal (won't win); acceleration of erosion clock
Crabclaw may formally annex some Fed Continuity districts

reset_conditions.description: Crabclaw internal collapse (three T7 warlords are uneasy)

interactions.description: completion accelerates Fed Continuity erosion
completion disrupts Delmarva exports northward briefly

crabclaw-fed-war.narrative_description: Alcott's attempt to re-impose federal authority over the Crabclaw lords failed. The three Baltimore warlords now know they do not have to bend. What they are learning is whether they can push. The clock is moving because Fed Continuity cannot accept the loss of Baltimore permanently and the Crabclaw lords cannot accept the loss of their independence.

advance_conditions.description: Preston personal demonstrations of force where challenged
incremental demand increases accepted by tributaries

completion_consequences.description: Hudson Valley polities (Bear-House, Yonkers Compact, several smaller) become formal vassals
Catskill Throne gains direct resource-requisition rights
Yonkers Compact internal crisis (was founded on horizontal non-subordination)
Bear-House of Peekskill may resist and fracture

interactions.description: completion changes regional power balance; Catskill Throne becomes clearly dominant in upper Hudson
Preston's personal mortality affects this clock's meaningfulness

catskill-throne-tribute.narrative_description: Tribute is already paid. The question is what the tribute means. Preston is slowly converting what began as protection-money into the formal language of lordship. Two segments filled. The Yonkers Three Judges have not yet had to decide whether they will kneel; the clock is what counts down to that decision.

advance_conditions.description: Fed Continuity weakness (removes anchor of status quo)

advance_rate.description: variable — 8 year spread across estimated firing window (T+3 to T+25)

completion_consequences.description: formal Species I polity declared
territorial claims in western Philadelphia and PG County corridor
regional political realignment
baseline-human factions either ally (Bourse, CJL) or resist (Iron Crown, Irregulars)

interactions.description: Fed Continuity erosion accelerates this
Bourse currency stability makes this more viable
Iron Crown aggression could force early declaration

species-i-consolidation.narrative_description: Elder Flores is old. Tomás Reyes is not. Between them is the question of whether Species I should be a people or a polity. The kin networks are already the largest coherent population in the mid-Atlantic. What they are not yet is a country. The clock ticks when Reyes wins an assembly argument, when Fed Continuity loses another district, when Iron Crown insults one of their own.

advance_conditions.description: Echo territorial expansion (not observed yet, but monitored)
appearance of additional anomalous entities in salvage zones

completion_consequences.description: anomalous entities push outside Financial District
Manhattan salvage economy disrupted
Tower Lords' primary revenue source threatened
regional response required; may accelerate eldritch-concerned faction alliances

interactions.description: completion hurts Staten Citadel (salvage dependent)
completion strengthens anti-eldritch faction alliances

manhattan-eldritch-breach.narrative_description: The Echo has not moved in four years. That is the comforting fact. The uncomfortable fact is that nobody knows what would move it, and the deep-salvage runs go further each year. Two segments of eight. The clock moves in small increments — a salvager goes in and doesn't come out, a building in the lower east appears to have shifted overnight, someone reports hearing something no one else heard. The clock does not move quickly. It only ever moves.

completion_consequences.description: unified Delmarva polity declared (under a single lord or council)
regional agricultural superpower emerges
Philadelphia Bourse pricing relationship changes
potential military action against Crabclaw or northward expansion

reset_conditions.description: dominant lord faction's patron dies without succession

interactions.description: completion transforms regional political map
completion gives Delmarva food-supply leverage equivalent to military power

harvest-lords-unification.narrative_description: The six major lords prefer their individual freedoms. None wants to kneel to another. None wants to be kneeled to, quite. One segment filled in four years. The clock moves when external pressure rises — when Crabclaw turns north, when Fed Continuity tries to levy them, when the Bourse proposes a price structure they can't refuse. If they unify, they will be the region's second great power after Catskill. If they never do, they will be the region's granary, which is perhaps enough.
